posts.title: Why I Think About System Design Like a Game Designer
posts.excerpt: After spending hundreds of hours in complex RPGs, I noticed something: the best game systems and the best software systems share a surprising amount of DNA. Both require elegant constraint, emergent behavior, and deep respect for the user's time. Here's what Elden Ring taught me about API design.

posts.title: The Hidden Cost of useEffect: A Deep Dive
posts.excerpt: Most React developers use useEffect daily without fully understanding its execution model. After profiling several production apps, I found that misused effects were responsible for some of the most subtle and painful bugs I've ever encountered. Let's fix that.

posts.title: What I Learned From Shipping Something Nobody Used
posts.excerpt: I spent three months building a product that got zero traction. Here's what I learned about validation, assumptions, and why "build it and they will come" is the most dangerous lie in software.

posts.title: Building a Personal CLI That Actually Gets Used
posts.excerpt: I've tried and abandoned dozens of productivity tools. The only ones that stick are the ones I build myself. Here's my approach to building CLI tools that become indispensable parts of your workflow.

posts.title: The Honest Engineer's Guide to Using AI Tools
posts.excerpt: There's a lot of noise around AI coding tools. After a year of daily use, here's my honest take on what they're genuinely good at, where they fail, and how I've restructured my workflow around their actual strengths and weaknesses.

sidebar.about: I'm Ming-Han, a software engineer who writes about engineering, career lessons, and occasionally what I'm playing. I aim to write things that are honest and technically useful.
